text.source_text: अत्राह — नानन्दमयः पर आत्मा भवितुमर्हति ; कस्मात् ? विकारशब्दात् । प्रकृतवचनादयमन्यः शब्दो विकारवचनः समधिगतः ‘ आनन्दमयः ’ इति , मयटो विकारार्थत्वात् । तस्मादन्नमयादिशब्दवद्विकारविषय एवानन्दमयशब्द इति चेत् , न ; प्राचुर्यार्थेऽपि मयटः स्मरणात् । ‘ तत्प्रकृतवचने मयट् ’ (पा. सू. ५ । ४ । २) इति हि प्रचुरतायामपि मयट् स्मर्यते ; यथा ‘ अन्नमयो यज्ञः ’ इत्यन्नप्रचुर उच्यते , एवमानन्दप्रचुरं ब्रह्मानन्दमयमुच्यते । आनन्दप्रचुरत्वं च ब्रह्मणो मनुष्यत्वादारभ्योत्तरस्मिन्नुत्तरस्मिन्स्थाने शतगुण आनन्द इत्युक्त्वा ब्रह्मानन्दस्य निरतिशयत्वावधारणात् । तस्मात्प्राचुर्यार्थे मयट् ॥ १३ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: atrāha — nānandamayaḥ para ātmā bhavitumarhati ; kasmāt ? vikāraśabdāt | prakṛtavacanādayamanyaḥ śabdo vikāravacanaḥ samadhigataḥ ‘ ānandamayaḥ ’ iti , mayaṭo vikārārthatvāt | tasmādannamayādiśabdavadvikāraviṣaya evānandamayaśabda iti cet , na ; prācuryārthe'pi mayaṭaḥ smaraṇāt | ‘ tatprakṛtavacane mayaṭ ’ (pā. sū. 5 | 4 | 2) iti hi pracuratāyāmapi mayaṭ smaryate ; yathā ‘ annamayo yajñaḥ ’ ityannapracura ucyate , evamānandapracuraṃ brahmānandamayamucyate | ānandapracuratvaṃ ca brahmaṇo manuṣyatvādārabhyottarasminnuttarasminsthāne śataguṇa ānanda ityuktvā brahmānandasya niratiśayatvāvadhāraṇāt | tasmātprācuryārthe mayaṭ || 13 ||

translation.literal_translation: Here one objects: anandamaya cannot be the Supreme Self. Why? Because it is a transformation-word. This term 'anandamaya,' occurring in the present context, is understood as denoting modification, since the suffix mayat can indicate vikara. Therefore, like annamaya etc., anandamaya too should denote a derivative entity. Reply: no, because mayat is also remembered in the sense of abundance (pracurya). Grammar teaches mayat in the sense of fullness too; as in 'annamayo yajna' meaning food-abundant, so Brahman rich in bliss is called anandamaya. And Brahman's abundance of bliss is established since, after describing each higher level as a hundredfold increase from human bliss upward, scripture finally determines Brahman-bliss as unsurpassed. Therefore here mayat is in the abundance sense. (13)
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.1.13 rebuts vikara reading by invoking mayat-pracurya usage; anandamaya denotes plenitude of bliss in Brahman, not a produced sheath-entity.
translation.interpretive_note: Suffix semantics is resolved by contextual excess-bliss gradation, not by defaulting to modification.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

A later philological pass should revisit mayat suffix semantics and anandamaya mantra-brahmana continuity framing in records 1.1.056-1.1.063.
